description_long: This is a large, vintage film projector with a robust, industrial design, likely constructed from metal and mounted on a heavy pedestal base. The main body is cylindrical with a prominent lens assembly protruding from one side, and two large circular film reel housings are attached to the upper and lower sections. The surface is painted in a muted gray color, showing signs of age and use, with various knobs, switches, and access panels distributed across its body. A small rectangular viewing window is present on the main housing, and a vented exhaust chimney extends from the top. The base is wide and flared for stability, with a control interface and wiring visible near the pedestal. This projector would have been used in cinemas or auditoriums for film projection, and its size and construction suggest it is a stationary, professional-grade device.
description: A large, vintage metal film projector with dual reel housings, a prominent lens, and a sturdy pedestal base, finished in muted gray.
description_view_0: Side view showing the narrow profile, with the lens and film reels aligned vertically and the pedestal base visible.
description_view_1: Full side view highlighting the dual film reel housings, lens assembly, control panel, and exhaust chimney on top.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the main housing, pedestal, and the alignment of the film reels.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with both film reels, lens assembly, and more detail of the mechanical components and wiring.
attribute_lens_type: fixed, large-diameter projection lens with adjustment rings
attribute_control_panel_design: A small control panel is located on the side of the pedestal base, featuring several mechanical switches, dials, and possibly indicator lights, with no digital interface visible.
attribute_ventilation_pattern: A single vented exhaust chimney is present on the top of the main housing, with additional small slotted vents on the sides and rear for airflow.
